```{r}
  theme_void()+theme(plot.background=element_rect(fill="#fdfcf8",color=NA))

# ── IS-LM ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_islm_b <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=is264,    aes(x=Y,y=r),color="black",  linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=lm0264,   aes(x=Y,y=r),color="black",  linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=lm1psp264,aes(x=Y,y=r),color="royalblue",    linewidth=lw264)+
  
  geom_line(data=lm1ssp264,aes(x=Y,y=r),color="red",    linewidth=lw264)+
  
  
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y0,    xend=Y0,    y=0,yend=r0),    linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,     xend=Y0,    y=r0,yend=r0),   linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y1_psp,xend=Y1_psp,y=0,yend=r1_psp),linetype="dotted",color="royalblue",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=Y1_psp,y=r1_psp,yend=r1_psp),linetype="dotted",color="royalblue",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y0,    y=r0),    size=1)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y1_psp,y=r1_psp),color="royalblue",    size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=15,y=93,label="IS",size=4,fontface="italic")+
  annotate("text",x=72,y=95,label=expression(LM(P["0,t"],pi["0,t+1"]^e)),size=4,color="black")+
  annotate("text",x=60,y=82,label=expression(LM(P["1,t"],pi["1,t+1"]^e)),size=4,color="royalblue")+
  annotate("text",x=85,y=52,label=expression(LM(P["0,t"],pi["1,t+1"]^e)),size=4,color="red")+
  annotate("text",x=Y0-3,y=-6,label=expression(Y["0,t"]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=Y1_psp+3, y=-6,label=expression(Y["1,t"]),size=4,color="red")+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=r0+3,label=expression(r["0,t"]),size=4,)+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=r1_psp-3,label=expression(r["1,t"]),              size=4,color="red")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(Y[t]),y=expression(r[t]))+th264

# ── Labor market ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_lab_b <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=nd264,aes(x=N,y=w),color=orange264,linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_line(data=ns264,aes(x=N,y=w),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N0,    xend=N0,    y=0,yend=w0),    linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,     xend=N0,    y=w0,yend=w0),   linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N1_psp,xend=N1_psp,y=0,yend=N1_psp),linetype="dotted",color="red",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=N1_psp,y=N1_psp,yend=N1_psp),linetype="dotted",color="red",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=N0,    y=w0),    size=1)+
  geom_point(aes(x=N1_psp,y=N1_psp),color="red",    size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=75,y=90,label=expression(N^s*(w[t])),                    size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=82,y=37,label=expression(N^d*(w[t]*","*A[t]*","*K[t])), size=4, color=orange264)+
  annotate("text",x=N0-2,y=-6,label=expression(N["0,t"]),size=4,)+
  annotate("text",x=N1_psp+2, y=-6,label=expression(N["1,t"]),                size=4,,color="red")+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=w0-2,label=expression(w["0,t"]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=N1_psp+2,label=expression(w["1,t"]),              size=4,color="red")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(N[t]),y=expression(w[t]))+th264

# ── AS-AD ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_asad_b <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=ad0264,aes(x=Y,y=P),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=ad1264,aes(x=Y,y=P),color="red",  linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=asp264,aes(x=Y,y=P),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  # NCL vertical AS at Y=50 (orange)
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y1_ncl,xend=Y1_ncl,y=0,yend=93),color=orange264,linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,    xend=Y0,    y=P0,    yend=P0),    linetype="dotted",color="black",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y0,   xend=Y0,    y=0,     yend=P0),    linetype="dotted",color="black",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,    xend=Y1_psp,y=P1_psp,yend=P1_psp),linetype="dotted",color="red",    linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y1_psp,xend=Y1_psp,y=0,    yend=100),linetype="dotted",color="red",    linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y0,    y=P0),    size=1)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y1_psp,y=P1_psp),color="red",    size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=15,  y=8, label="AS",size=4,fontface="italic")+
  annotate("text",x=Y1_ncl-10,y=95,label=expression(AS^f),size=4,hjust=0,color=orange264)+
  annotate("text",x=85, y=8,label=expression(AD[0]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=85, y=30,label=expression(AD[1]),size=4,color="red")+
  annotate("text",x=Y0-3,y=-6,label=expression(Y["0,t"]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=Y1_psp+3, y=-6,label=expression(Y["1,t"]),  size=4,,color="red")+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=P0-3,label=expression(P["0,t"]),  size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=P1_psp+3, label=expression(P["1,t"]),  size=4,color="red")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(Y[t]),y=expression(P[t]))+th264

# ── Production function ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_pf_b <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=pf264,aes(x=N,y=Y),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N0,    xend=N0,    y=0,yend=100),    linetype="dotted",color="black",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N0,    xend=100,   y=Y0,yend=Y0),   linetype="dotted",color="black",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N1_psp,xend=N1_psp,y=0,yend=100),linetype="dotted",color="red",    linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N1_psp,xend=100,   y=Y1_psp,yend=Y1_psp),linetype="dotted",color="red",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=N0,    y=Y0),    size=1)+
  geom_point(aes(x=N1_psp,y=Y1_psp),color="red",    size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=80,y=80,label=expression(A[t]*F(K[t],N[t])),size=4,hjust=0)+
  annotate("text",x=N0-3,y=-6,label=expression(N["0,t"]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=N1_psp+3, y=-6,label=expression(N["1,t"]),                size=4,color="red")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(N[t]),y=expression(Y[t]))+th264

# ── 45-degree ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_45_b <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y0,    xend=Y0,    y=0,yend=100),    linetype="dotted",color="black",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,     xend=Y0,    y=Y0,yend=Y0),   linetype="dotted",color="black",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y1_psp,xend=Y1_psp,y=0,yend=100),linetype="dotted",color="red",    linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=Y1_psp,y=Y1_psp,yend=Y1_psp),linetype="dotted",color="red",    linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y0,    y=Y0),    size=1)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y1_psp,y=Y1_psp),color="red",    size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=85,y=73,label=expression(Y[t]==Y[t]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=Y0-3,y=-6,label=expression(Y["0,t"]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=Y1_psp+3, y=-6,label=expression(Y["1,t"]),                size=4,color="red")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(Y[t]),y=expression(Y[t]))+th264

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p_leg_b,p_islm_b,p_lab_b,p_asad_b,p_pf_b,p_45_b,nrow=3,ncol=2)
```

---

### Part (c) — Neoclassical Model

With prices fully flexible, the supply side alone determines output. Since
$\pi^e_{t+1}$ leaves the IS curve and the labor market completely unchanged, the
supply-determined output $Y_t^*$ does not move.

**Mechanism:** The rightward LM shift also shifts AD right which, given $\Rightarrow$ vertical AS (supply-determined $Y_t^*$unchanged) $\Rightarrow$ $P_t\uparrow$. In turn,  a higher $P_t$ reduces real balances, shifting LM back left $\Rightarrow$ the two LM effects (rightward from $\pi^e_{t+1}$, leftward from $P_t\uparrow$) exactly cancel $\Rightarrow$ $r_t$ unchanged.

**Labor market, consumption, investment:** All unchanged. The shock has no real effects in the Neoclassical model — only the price level rises proportionally to absorb the excess money demand created by the expected inflation. The real interest rate is pinned by the supply side and the IS; the nominal interest rate $i_t = r_t + \pi^e_{t+1}$ rises by the full amount of the shock (the Fisher effect).

```{r}
#| label: fig-ex264-ncl
#| fig-cap: "Neoclassical model: $\\pi^e_{t+1}$ increase. Black = original = post-shock real equilibrium (unchanged). Red dashed = intermediate LM position at original price level $P_0$, illustrating the initial rightward LM shift before prices adjust. In equilibrium $P_t$ rises from 50 to 55, shifting the LM back to its original position; all real variables are unchanged."
#| fig-alt: "Six-panel Neoclassical diagram. IS-LM: shows initial LM shift right (red dashed) and final return to original LM; equilibrium Y and r unchanged. AS-AD: AD shifts right along vertical AS; P rises, Y unchanged. All other panels show single unchanged equilibrium."
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-width: 7

# ── Legend ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_leg_c <- ggplot()+
  annotate("segment",x=5,xend=22,y=85,yend=85,color="black",  linewidth=1.2)+
  annotate("segment",x=5,xend=22,y=70,yend=70,color="red",    linewidth=1.2)+
  annotate("segment",x=5,xend=22,y=55,yend=55,color="royalblue",    linewidth=1.2)+
  
  annotate("text",x=26,y=85,label="Original",hjust=0,size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=26,y=70,label="Post-shock",hjust=0,size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=26,y=55,label="Post-shock indir. P effect on LM",hjust=0,size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=5, y=43,label="0 subscript: Original",hjust=0,size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=5, y=27,label="1 subscript: Post-shock",  hjust=0,size=4)+
  xlim(0,100)+ylim(0,100)+
  theme_void()+theme(plot.background=element_rect(fill="#fdfcf8",color=NA))

# ── IS-LM ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_islm_c <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=is264,    aes(x=Y,y=r),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=lm0264,   aes(x=Y,y=r),color="royalblue",linewidth=lw264)+
  # Intermediate LM at P=50 after shock (red)
  geom_line(data=lm1init264,aes(x=Y,y=r),color="red",linewidth=lw264,)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y0,xend=Y0,y=0,yend=r0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0, xend=Y0,y=r0,yend=r0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y0,y=r0),size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=15,y=93,label="IS",size=4,fontface="italic")+
  
  annotate("text",x=60,y=95,label=expression(LM(P["0,t"],pi["0,t+1"]^e)),size=4,color="black")+
  annotate("text",x=60,y=88,label=expression("="),size=4,color="black")+
  
  annotate("text",x=60,y=83,label=expression(LM(P["1,t"],pi["1,t+1"]^e)),size=4,color="royalblue")+
  
  annotate("text",x=85,y=50,label=expression(LM(P["0,t"],pi["1,t+1"]^e)),size=4,color="red")+
  
  
  annotate("text",x=Y0,y=-6,label=expression(Y["0,t"]*"="*Y["1,t"]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=-8,y=r0,label=expression(r["0,t"]*"="*r["1,t"]),size=4)+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(Y[t]),y=expression(r[t]))+th264

# ── Labor market (unchanged) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_lab_c <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=nd264,aes(x=N,y=w),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=ns264,aes(x=N,y=w),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N0,xend=N0,y=0,yend=w0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0, xend=N0,y=w0,yend=w0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=N0,y=w0),size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=78,y=90,label=expression(N^s*(w[t])),                    size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=90,y=30,label=expression(N^d*(w[t]*","*A[t]*","*K[t])), size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=N0,y=-6,label=expression(N["0,t"]*"="*N["1,t"]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=-8,y=w0,label=expression(w["0,t"]*"="*w["1,t"]),size=3)+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(N[t]),y=expression(w[t]))+th264

# ── AS-AD ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_asad_c <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=ad0264,aes(x=Y,y=P),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=ad1264,aes(x=Y,y=P),color="red",  linewidth=lw264)+
  # Vertical AS at Y=50 (unchanged)
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y0,xend=Y0,y=0,yend=93),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=Y0,y=P0,    yend=P0),    linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=Y0,y=P1_ncl,yend=P1_ncl),linetype="dotted",color="red",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y0,y=P0),    size=1)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y0,y=P1_ncl),color="red",size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=Y0+5,y=95,label="AS",size=4,fontface="italic")+
  annotate("text",x=85, y=5, label=expression(AD[0]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=85, y=30, label=expression(AD[1]),size=4,color="red")+
  annotate("text",x=Y0,y=-6,  label=expression(Y["0,t"]*"="*Y["1,t"]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=P0-2,label=expression(P["0,t"]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=P1_ncl+2, label=expression(P["1,t"]),size=4,color="red")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(Y[t]),y=expression(P[t]))+th264

# ── Production function (unchanged) ──────────────────────────────────────────
p_pf_c <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=pf264,aes(x=N,y=Y),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N0,xend=N0, y=0,yend=100),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N0,xend=100,y=Y0,yend=Y0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=N0,y=Y0),size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=80,y=75,label=expression(A[t]*F(K[t],N[t])),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=N0,y=-6,label=expression(N["0,t"]*"="*N["1,t"]),size=4)+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(N[t]),y=expression(Y[t]))+th264

# ── 45-degree (unchanged) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_45_c <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y0,xend=Y0,y=0,yend=100), linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0, xend=Y0,y=Y0,yend=Y0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y0,y=Y0),size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=85,y=70,label=expression(Y[t]==Y[t]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=Y0,y=-6,label=expression(Y["0,t"]*"="*Y["1,t"]),size=4)+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(Y[t]),y=expression(Y[t]))+th264

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p_leg_c,p_islm_c,p_lab_c,p_asad_c,p_pf_c,p_45_c,nrow=3,ncol=2)
```

---

### Summary

| Variable | Simple Sticky Price | Partial Sticky Price | Neoclassical |
|----------|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| $\Delta Y_t$ | $\uparrow\uparrow$ (largest) | $\uparrow$ | $0$ |
| $\Delta P_t$ | $0$ | $\uparrow$ | $\uparrow\uparrow$ (largest) |
| $\Delta r_t$ | $\downarrow\downarrow$ | $\downarrow$ | $0$ |
| $\Delta N_t$ | $\uparrow\uparrow$ | $\uparrow$ | $0$ |
| $\Delta w_t$ | $\downarrow\downarrow$ | $\downarrow$ | $0$ |
| $\Delta C_t$ | $\uparrow\uparrow$ | $\uparrow$ | $0$ |
| $\Delta I_t$ | $\uparrow\uparrow$ | $\uparrow$ | $0$ |

This is a **pure demand shock** (monetary channel). Unlike the $\theta_t$ supply shock in
Exercise 26.2, here greater price flexibility means the price level absorbs more of
the adjustment, leaving real variables unchanged. In the Neoclassical model, the
Fisher effect operates fully: $i_t = r_t + \pi^e_{t+1}$ rises by the full amount of the
shock, $P_t$ rises proportionally, and all real quantities are unchanged. In the SSP
model, prices cannot adjust, so the entire effect falls on real output and the real
interest rate.

---

# Exercise 26.5 — CB Policy for Constant Real Wage: $A_{t+1}\uparrow$ in the PSP Model

**Problem.** Suppose that we have a partial sticky-price New Keynesian model. Suppose
that the economy is hit with an increase in $A_{t+1}$. Suppose that the central bank
wants to adjust the money supply in such a way that the real wage does not change in
response to this shock.

**(a)** How must the central bank adjust policy in response to the increase in
$A_{t+1}$ in order to achieve this end?

**(b)** How does output react to the change in $A_{t+1}$ if the central bank follows
such a policy?

**(c)** How does this change in $Y_t$ compare to a world in which the money supply is
exogenous (i.e. does not react to the increase in $A_{t+1}$)?

---

## Solution

### How $A_{t+1}\uparrow$ affects the economy

$A_{t+1}$ (expected future total factor productivity) enters **investment demand**:

$$I_t = I^d(r_t,\; A_{t+1},\; K_t), \qquad \partial I^d/\partial A_{t+1} > 0.$$

Higher expected productivity raises the return on capital investment today, directly
increasing $I_t$ for any given $r_t$. This shifts the **IS curve to the right**.

**Key fact.** $A_{t+1}$ does not affect: (i) current labor demand $N^d$ (which depends on
$A_t$, not $A_{t+1}$), (ii) labor supply $N^s$, (iii) the LM curve, or (iv) the current production function. This is a **pure demand-side (IS) shock** with no direct supply-side channel.

In the AD–AS diagram: IS shifts right $\Rightarrow$ for any $P_t$, IS–LM gives higher
$Y_t$ $\Rightarrow$ **AD shifts right**. With the upward-sloping AS (unaffected by
$A_{t+1}$), both $Y_t$ and $P_t$ rise. Employment rises and so does the real wage rate (movement along $N^s$). This is shown in Figure (a) below.

### Why the CB must reduce $M_t$

The real wage is determined by the labor supply curve. This means $w_t$ is constant if and only if $N_t$ is constant. Since $A_t$ and $K_t$ are unaffected this is equivalent to output $Y_t$ being constant. Therefore, to keep wages constant, 
the CB must prevent output from rising. Without intervention, AD shifts right and the
 equilibrium moves to higher $Y_t$. To restore the original equilibrium, the CB must
shift AD back to its original position. Since IS shifted right, the CB must shift LM
to the **left** by the same amount — which requires **contracting** (reducing) the
money supply $M_t$.

```{r}
#| label: setup-ex265
#| include: false

# IS₀: r=100-Y; IS₁: r=120-Y (A_{t+1} shock, rightward shift of 20)
# LM₀ at P: r=Y+2P-110 (original money supply)
# AD₀: P=100-Y;  AD₁_nobc: P=115-Y;  AD₁_cb = AD₀ (CB restores AD)
# PSP AS: P=Y  (same as Ex 26.2)

# ── No-CB PSP equilibrium ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# AD₁_nobc ∩ AS_psp: 115-Y = Y → Y=57.5, P=57.5
# IS₁ at Y=57.5: r = 120-57.5 = 62.5
Y1n <- 57.5;  r1n <- 62.5;  P1n <- 57.5
N1n <- Y1n^2 / 50          # 66.125
w1n <- 100 - N1n            # 33.875
lm_cn <- 2*P1n - 110        # 5  (LM at P=57.5: r=Y+5)

# ── CB PSP equilibrium ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# CB shifts LM left until AD = AD₀; equilibrium: (Y₀, P₀) unchanged
# IS₁ at Y=50: r=70 — this is the new r under CB policy
Y1c <- 50;  r1c <- 70;  P1c <- 50;  N1c <- 50;  w1c <- 50
lm_cc <- 20   # LM₁_cb at P=50: r = Y+20

# ── Data frames (seq264, ax264, lw264, th264 from setup-ex264) ─────────────────
is1_265     <- tibble(Y=seq264, r=120-Y)         |> filter(r>=0, r<=100)
lm1n_265    <- tibble(Y=seq264, r=Y+lm_cn)       |> filter(r>=0, r<=100, Y>=0)
lm1c_265    <- tibble(Y=seq264, r=Y+lm_cc)       |> filter(r>=0, r<=100, Y>=0)
ad1n_265    <- tibble(Y=seq264, P=115-Y)         |> filter(P>=0, Y>=0)
```

---

### Part (a) — Without CB Intervention (exogenous $M_t$)

IS shifts right $\Rightarrow$ AD shifts right $\Rightarrow$  $Y_t\uparrow$,
$P_t\uparrow$, $r_t\uparrow$, $N_t\uparrow$, $w_t\uparrow$.

- **Consumption:** $r_t\uparrow$ depresses $C_t$ (intertemporal substitution); $Y_t\uparrow$
  raises $C_t$ (income). Net effect ambiguous, depends on magnitudes.
- **Investment:** $A_{t+1}\uparrow$ raises $I_t$ directly; $r_t\uparrow$ partially offsets. Since output increases, this means consumption plus investment must increase ($G_t$ is unchnaged).
- **Real wage:** $w_t\uparrow$ — higher output pushes up $N_t$, raising the wage rate along the upward-sloping $N^s$.

```{r}
#| label: fig-ex265-nobc
#| fig-cap: "PSP model without CB intervention: $A_{t+1}$ increase. Black = original; red = post-shock. IS shifts right (higher investment demand). AD shifts right. Along the upward-sloping AS, $Y_t$, $P_t$, and $r_t$ all rise; $N_t$ rises and $w_t$ rises."
#| fig-alt: "Six-panel PSP diagram, no CB. IS-LM: IS shifts right, LM shifts left slightly as P rises, output and real rate both rise. AS-AD: AD shifts right along upward-sloping AS, Y and P rise. Labor market: equilibrium moves right along Ns to higher N and higher w. PF and 45-degree confirm higher output."
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-width: 7

# ── Legend ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_leg_n265 <- ggplot()+
  annotate("segment",x=5,xend=22,y=85,yend=85,color="black",linewidth=1.2)+
  annotate("segment",x=5,xend=22,y=70,yend=70,color="red",  linewidth=1.2)+
  annotate("segment",x=5,xend=22,y=55,yend=55,color="royalblue",  linewidth=1.2)+
  annotate("text",x=26,y=85,label="Original",   hjust=0,size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=26,y=70,label="Post-shock (no CB)",hjust=0,size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=26,y=55,label="Post-shock, indir. P effect on LM",hjust=0,size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=5, y=35,label="0: Original",  hjust=0,size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=5, y=20,label="1: Post-shock",hjust=0,size=4)+
  xlim(0,100)+ylim(0,100)+
  theme_void()+theme(plot.background=element_rect(fill="#fdfcf8",color=NA))

# ── IS-LM ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_islm_n265 <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=is264,    aes(x=Y,y=r),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=is1_265,  aes(x=Y,y=r),color="red",  linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=lm0264,   aes(x=Y,y=r),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=lm1n_265, aes(x=Y,y=r),color="royalblue",  linewidth=lw264)+
  # Drop lines — baseline
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y0,xend=Y0,y=0,yend=r0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0, xend=Y0,y=r0,yend=r0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  # Drop lines — no-CB
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y1n,xend=Y1n,y=0,yend=r1n),linetype="dotted",color="red",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,  xend=Y1n,y=r1n,yend=r1n),linetype="dotted",color="red",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y0, y=r0), size=1)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y1n,y=r1n),color="red",size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=12,y=73, label=expression(IS(A["0,t+1"])),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=37,y=98, label=expression(IS(A["1,t+1"])),size=4,color="red")+
  annotate("text",x=80,y=65, label=expression(LM(P["0,t"])),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=70,y=90, label=expression(LM(P["1,t"])),size=4,color="royalblue")+
  annotate("text",x=Y0, y=-6,label=expression(Y["0,t"]),size=4,vjust=1)+
  annotate("text",x=Y1n+1,y=-6,label=expression(Y["1,t"]),size=4,vjust=1,color="red")+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=r0,      label=expression(r["0,t"]),size=4,hjust=1)+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=r1n+1.5, label=expression(r["1,t"]),size=4,hjust=1,color="red")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(Y[t]),y=expression(r[t]))+th264

# ── Labor market ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_lab_n265 <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=ns264,aes(x=N,y=w),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N0, xend=N0, y=0,yend=w0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,  xend=N0, y=w0,yend=w0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N1n,xend=N1n,y=0,yend=N1n),linetype="dotted",color="red",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,  xend=N1n,y=N1n,yend=N1n),linetype="dotted",color="red",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=N0, y=w0), size=1)+
  geom_point(aes(x=N1n,y=N1n),color="red",size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=78,y=90,label=expression(N^s*(w[t])),                   size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=N0,  y=-6,label=expression(N["0,t"]),size=4,vjust=1)+
  annotate("text",x=N1n+1,y=-6,label=expression(N["1,t"]),size=4,vjust=1,color="red")+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=w0-1,label=expression(w["0,t"]),size=4,hjust=1)+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=N1n+1, label=expression(w["1,t"]),size=4,hjust=1,color="red")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(N[t]),y=expression(w[t]))+th264

# ── AS-AD ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_asad_n265 <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=ad0264,  aes(x=Y,y=P),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=ad1n_265,aes(x=Y,y=P),color="red",  linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=asp264,  aes(x=Y,y=P),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,  xend=Y0, y=P0, yend=P0), linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y0, xend=Y0, y=0,  yend=100), linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,  xend=Y1n,y=P1n,yend=P1n),linetype="dotted",color="red",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y1n,xend=Y1n,y=0,  yend=100),linetype="dotted",color="red",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y0, y=P0), size=1)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y1n,y=P1n),color="red",size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=5, y=20, label=expression(AS[0]),size=4,hjust=0)+
  annotate("text",x=85,y=5,label=expression(AD[0]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=85,y=40,label=expression(AD[1]),size=4,color="red")+
  annotate("text",x=Y0,  y=-6,label=expression(Y["0,t"]),size=4,vjust=1)+
  annotate("text",x=Y1n+1,y=-6,label=expression(Y["1,t"]),size=4,vjust=1,color="red")+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=P0-1,      label=expression(P["0,t"]),size=4,hjust=1)+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=P1n+1, label=expression(P["1,t"]),size=4,hjust=1,color="red")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(Y[t]),y=expression(P[t]))+th264

# ── Production function ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_pf_n265 <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=pf264,aes(x=N,y=Y),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N0, xend=N0, y=0,yend=100),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N0, xend=100,y=Y0,yend=Y0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N1n,xend=N1n,y=0,yend=100),linetype="dotted",color="red",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N1n,xend=100,y=Y1n,yend=Y1n),linetype="dotted",color="red",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=N0, y=Y0), size=1)+
  geom_point(aes(x=N1n,y=Y1n),color="red",size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=75,y=80,label=expression(A[t]*F(K[t],N[t])),size=4,hjust=0)+
  annotate("text",x=N0,  y=-6,label=expression(N["0,t"]),size=4,vjust=1)+
  annotate("text",x=N1n+1,y=-6,label=expression(N["1,t"]),size=4,vjust=1,color="red")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(N[t]),y=expression(Y[t]))+th264

# ── 45-degree ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_45_n265 <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y0, xend=Y0, y=0,yend=100), linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,  xend=Y0, y=Y0,yend=Y0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y1n,xend=Y1n,y=0,yend=100),linetype="dotted",color="red",  linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,  xend=Y1n,y=Y1n,yend=Y1n),linetype="dotted",color="red",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y0, y=Y0), size=1)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y1n,y=Y1n),color="red",size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=85,y=73,label=expression(Y[t]==Y[t]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=Y0,  y=-6,label=expression(Y["0,t"]),size=4,vjust=1)+
  annotate("text",x=Y1n+1,y=-6,label=expression(Y["1,t"]),size=4,vjust=1,color="red")+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(Y[t]),y=expression(Y[t]))+th264

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p_leg_n265,p_islm_n265,p_lab_n265,
                        p_asad_n265,p_pf_n265,p_45_n265,nrow=3,ncol=2)
```

---

### Part (b) — With CB Intervention (constant $w_t$)

The CB reduces $M_t$, shifting LM to the left. It reduces $M_t$ by exactly the amount
needed so that the new IS–LM intersection gives **the same AD curve as before the
shock**: $AD_1^{CB} = AD_0$. In the new equilibrium, output and the price level are
unchanged: $Y_t = Y_{0,t}$, $P_t = P_{0,t}$, $N_t = N_{0,t}$, $w_t = w_{0,t}$.

**What changes is only the real interest rate:** IS shifted right and LM shifted left, so their intersection occurs at the original $Y_t$ but at ahigher $r_t$.

- **Real interest rate:** $r_t\uparrow$ (IS right + LM left → same $Y$, higher $r$).
- **Consumption:** $r_t\uparrow$ reduces $C_t$.
- **Investment:** $A_{t+1}\uparrow$ raises desired investment, but $r_t\uparrow$ offsets it partially through the IS equilibrium condition. By construction of the IS, the goods market clears at $Y_{0,t}$ with higher $r_t$: the additional investment demand from $A_{t+1}$ is crowded out by the rise in the real interest rate induced by the CB's   contraction.
- **Real wage:** unchanged by construction.

The AS–AD diagram ends up **unchanged** — the CB's action keeps the AD curve at
$AD_0$, so the AS–AD intersection remains at $(Y_{0,t},\,P_{0,t})$. The red
curve shows where $AD_1$ would be without the CB intervention.

```{r}
#| label: fig-ex265-cb
#| fig-cap: "PSP model with CB intervention (constant $w_t$ target): $A_{t+1}$ increase. IS₁ shifts right (shock); LM₁ shifts left (CB money contraction); their intersection occurs at the original $Y_t$ but a higher $r_t$. In the AS–AD panel, the shock brings $AD$ out (red) and the money stock contraction brings it back to its original position (green)."
#| fig-alt: "Six-panel CB-intervention diagram. IS-LM: IS shifts right (red), LM shifts left (dark-green), new equilibrium has same Y but higher r. AS-AD: AD shifts right (red) and monetary contraction shifts it back to equilibrium at original Y and P. Labor market, PF, 45-degree all show single unchanged equilibrium."
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-width: 7

cb_col <- "darkgreen"

# ── Legend ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_leg_c265 <- ggplot()+
  annotate("segment",x=5,xend=22,y=85,yend=85,color="black",   linewidth=1.2)+
  annotate("segment",x=5,xend=22,y=70,yend=70,color="red",     linewidth=1.2)+
  annotate("segment",x=5,xend=22,y=55,yend=55,color=cb_col,    linewidth=1.2)+
  annotate("text",x=26,y=85,label="Original",hjust=0,size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=26,y=70,label="Shock",hjust=0,size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=26,y=55,label="CB policy",        hjust=0,size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=5, y=26,label="0: Original",    hjust=0,size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=5, y=12,label="1,CB: post-shock with CB",   hjust=0,size=4)+
  xlim(0,100)+ylim(0,100)+
  theme_void()+theme(plot.background=element_rect(fill="#fdfcf8",color=NA))

# ── IS-LM ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_islm_c265 <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=is264,    aes(x=Y,y=r),color="black", linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=is1_265,  aes(x=Y,y=r),color="red",  linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=lm0264,   aes(x=Y,y=r),color="black", linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=lm1c_265, aes(x=Y,y=r),color=cb_col,  linewidth=lw264)+
  # Drop lines — baseline
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y0,xend=Y0,y=0,yend=r0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0, xend=Y0,y=r0,yend=r0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  # Drop lines — CB (same Y, higher r)
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y1c,xend=Y1c,y=0,yend=r1c),linetype="dotted",color=cb_col,linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,  xend=Y1c,y=r1c,yend=r1c),linetype="dotted",color=cb_col,linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y0, y=r0), size=1)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y1c,y=r1c),color=cb_col,size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=12,y=75, label=expression(IS(A["0,t+1"])),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=40,y=95, label=expression(IS(A["1,t+1"])),size=4,color="red")+
  annotate("text",x=85,y=70, label=expression(LM(M["0,t"])),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=50,y=84, label=expression(LM(M["1,t"])),size=4,color=cb_col)+
  annotate("text",x=Y0,  y=-6,label=expression(Y["0,t"]*"="*Y["1,t"]^CB),size=4,vjust=1)+
  annotate("text",x=-4,y=r0,      label=expression(r["0,t"]),size=4,hjust=1)+
  annotate("text",x=-4,y=r1c+1.5, label=expression(r["1,t"]^CB),size=4,hjust=1,color=cb_col)+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(Y[t]),y=expression(r[t]))+th264

# ── Labor market (unchanged) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_lab_c265 <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=ns264,aes(x=N,y=w),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N0,xend=N0,y=0,yend=w0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0, xend=N0,y=w0,yend=w0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=N0,y=w0),size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=78,y=90,label=expression(N^s*(w[t])),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=N0,y=-6,label=expression(N["0,t"]*"="*N["1,t"]^CB),size=4,vjust=1)+
  annotate("text",x=-6,y=w0,label=expression(w["0,t"]*"="*w["1,t"]^CB),size=3)+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(N[t]),y=expression(w[t]))+th264

# ── AS-AD (CB keeps AD = AD₀; dashed red = counterfactual no-CB) ─────────────
p_asad_c265 <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=ad0264,  aes(x=Y,y=P),color=cb_col,linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=ad1n_265,aes(x=Y,y=P),color="red",  linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_line(data=asp264,  aes(x=Y,y=P),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0, xend=Y0,y=P0,yend=P0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y0,xend=Y0,y=0, yend=100),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y0,y=P0),size=1, color=cb_col)+
  annotate("text",x=5, y=20, label=expression(AS),size=4,hjust=0)+
  annotate("text",x=65,y=15,label=expression(AD[0]*"="*AD[1]^CB),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=85,y=40,label=expression(AD[1]),size=4,color="red")+
  annotate("text",x=Y0,y=-6,label=expression(Y["0,t"]*"="*Y["1,t"]^CB),size=4,vjust=1)+
  annotate("text",x=-4,y=P0,label=expression(P["0,t"]*"="*P["1,t"]^CB),size=4,hjust=1)+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(Y[t]),y=expression(P[t]))+th264

# ── Production function (unchanged) ──────────────────────────────────────────
p_pf_c265 <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_line(data=pf264,aes(x=N,y=Y),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N0,xend=N0, y=0,yend=100),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=N0,xend=100,y=Y0,yend=Y0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=N0,y=Y0),size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=70,y=80,label=expression(A[t]*F(K[t],N[t])),size=4,hjust=0)+
  annotate("text",x=N0,y=-6,label=expression(N["0,t"]*"="*N["1,t"]^CB),size=4,vjust=1)+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(N[t]),y=expression(Y[t]))+th264

# ── 45-degree (unchanged) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_45_c265 <- ggplot()+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=0),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=0,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=0.4)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0,xend=100,y=0,yend=100),color="black",linewidth=lw264)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=Y0,xend=Y0,y=0,yend=100), linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_segment(aes(x=0, xend=Y0,y=Y0,yend=Y0),linetype="dotted",color="black",linewidth=0.5)+
  geom_point(aes(x=Y0,y=Y0),size=1)+
  annotate("text",x=85,y=70,label=expression(Y[t]==Y[t]),size=4)+
  annotate("text",x=Y0,y=-6,label=expression(Y["0,t"]*"="*Y["1,t"]^CB),size=4,vjust=1)+
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-ax264,100),ylim=c(-ax264,100),clip="off")+
  labs(x=expression(Y[t]),y=expression(Y[t]))+th264

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p_leg_c265,p_islm_c265,p_lab_c265,
                        p_asad_c265,p_pf_c265,p_45_c265,nrow=3,ncol=2)
```

---

### Part (c) — Comparison

**With exogenous money supply** (no CB intervention): $A_{t+1}\uparrow$ shifts IS and
AD right $\Rightarrow$ Equilibrium: $Y_t\uparrow$, $P_t\uparrow$, $r_t\uparrow$,
$N_t\uparrow$, $w_t\uparrow$.

**With CB policy** (constant $w_t$ target): $Y_t$ does not change at all. The CB
contracts $M_t$ to fully offset the AD shift. Output is completely insulated from the
$A_{t+1}$ shock.

The cost of this stabilization is a higher real interest rate $r_t$: both the
IS shift (rightward) and the CB's LM contraction (leftward) push $r_t$ up at the given $Y_t$. Consumption falls (higher $r_t$) by the same absolute amount of the increase in investment (which is pulled up by $A_{t+1}$ and down by the interest-rate response).

| Variable | Exogenous $M_t$ (no CB) | CB policy ($\Delta w_t=0$) |
|----------|:---:|:---:|
| $\Delta Y_t$ | $\uparrow$ | $0$ |
| $\Delta P_t$ | $\uparrow$ | $0$ |
| $\Delta r_t$ | $\uparrow$ | $\uparrow\uparrow$ (much larger) |
| $\Delta N_t$ | $\uparrow$ | $0$ |
| $\Delta w_t$ | $\downarrow$ | $0$ (by design) |
| $\Delta C_t$ | ambiguous | $\downarrow$ ($r_t\uparrow$, $Y_t$ unchanged) |
| $\Delta I_t$ | $\uparrow$ (net) | $\uparrow$ (net) |
